text:
Welcome to CLUE!

CLUE is a platform that helps your class figure things out together as a community of scientists and engineers. On clue you can work individually and in a group, and you can share your ideas with the class and see other people's ideas.

In CLUE, everyone works in a group with up to four people. When you start an activity, you can choose a group. You can change groups by clicking on the group number in the upper right corner. Then, you can either join an existing group that has less than four students, or start a new group.

Right now, make sure you are in the same group as the other students you are sitting and working with. If your groupmates need help, help them! You're figuring things out together.

Each lesson in CLUE has three parts. The orange tabs above show each part.

  • Introduction
  • Activities
  • Reflection

When you're done with one part, go to the next one.

objects.text: When you hover your mouse over any tile in CLUE, a little triangle will appear that will let you pick up the tile and move it to your work space.

text:
Whenever you see a blue question tile like this, you know you need to answer a question.

Try it out now! Copy this to your workspace and answer the question below.

If your class is using handouts, answer this question on your handout instead!

When you drag the questions tile over, you'll be able to write your responses here.

What is something that you think scientists and engineers worked together on?

How do you think the scientists contributed, and how do you think the engineers contributed?
